blocks.text: 3.4.1 REMOVAL AND INSTALLATION
Removal Procedure
Step 1 Remove the floor mat and rear toe board.
Step 2 Remove the front toe board.
Step 3 Remove the propeller shaft cover. (UPPER)
( Except Cu 35 to 70 model)
Step 4 Remove the propeller shaft set bolt.
Step 5 Remove the propeller shaft cover. (LOWER)
(Except Pn 60 to 80 model)
Step 6 Remove the propeller shaft set bolt.
Step 7 Remove the propeller shaft.
Installation Procedure
The installation procedure is the reverse of the removal procedure.
## NOTICE
**Add MP grease through the grease fitting.**
3.4.2 DISASSEMBLY AND INSPECTION AND REASSEMBLY(Pn35 to 45,Cu35 to 50 MODEL)
### Disassembly Procedure
Step 1 Fix the propeller shaft in a vise.
Step 2 Remove the snap rings.
Step 3 Remove the universal spider sets.
• Use a brass bar and extract the bearing cup
• Use the brass bar to extract the bearing cup on the other side and remove the spider.
• Remove the spider set on the opposite side in the same way.
Reassembly Procedure
The reassembly procedure is the reverse of the disassembly procedure.
Step 3 Install the universal spider sets.
• Coat MP grease on the needle roller in the bearing cup.
• Use a brass bar and drive in the bearing cup a little.
• Install the spider.
• Use the brass bar and drive in the bearing cup until the snap ring groove appears.
• Use the brass bar and drive in the bearing cup on the opposite side until the snap ring groove appears.
• Install the spider set on the opposite side in the same way.

figures.description: View into the forklift compartment showing the upper propeller shaft cover being removed.
Underside view of the propeller shaft set bolts being removed.
View of the lower propeller shaft cover and its bolt being removed.
Close-up of the propeller shaft set bolts at the joint flange being removed.
Exploded view of the universal joint disassembly, showing the spider sets and the snap rings separated from the yoke.
Removing a universal joint bearing cup by tapping it out with a brass bar and hammer while the propeller shaft is held in a vise.
Extracting the bearing cup on the opposite side with a brass bar to free the spider from the yoke.
Driving a bearing cup back into the yoke with a brass bar and hammer during universal joint reassembly.
Driving the bearing cup into the opposite side of the yoke with a brass bar during reassembly.
